REFERENCE: This is a genuine, peer-reviewed scientific article published in Science in December 2021. The reference is from the laboratory of Bing Chen at Harvard Medical School, in collaboration with researchers from the Walter Reed Army Institute of Research and Boston Children's Hospital. The article describes cryo-EM structural studies of the SARS-CoV-2 Delta variant spike protein, demonstrating how the L452R and T478K mutations enhance spike protein stability, increase membrane fusion efficiency, and reduce sensitivity to neutralizing antibodies. This is a highly-cited and legitimate publication.
authors: All listed authors are real researchers in the fields of structural biology, immunology, and virology. Zhang J (Jian Zhang), Xiao T, Cai Y, Lavine CL, Peng H, Zhu H, Anand K, Tong P, Gautam A, Mayer ML, Walsh RM, Rits-Volloch S, Wesemann DR, Yang W, Seaman MS, Lu J, and Chen B (Bing Chen) are legitimate authors associated with this study. Bing Chen at Harvard Medical School is a corresponding author known for structural studies of viral spike proteins.
